issues.explanation: Using current spacecraft distance (18 billion km from Earth in 2026) instead of mission destination (Jupiter at 778M km)
Using current spacecraft distance instead of Saturn flyby distance (1,275M km)
Using current interstellar distance (24B km) instead of Saturn encounter (1,275M km). Could also use Jupiter (778M km) as primary target.
Using current distance instead of Neptune flyby (4,500M km). Grand Tour visited Jupiter (778), Saturn (1,275), Uranus (2,870), Neptune (4,500).
Mission has not launched as of Feb 2026. Currently scheduled for 2026 or later.
issues.fix: Remove this entry or mark as 'planned' with tentative year 2026+

issues.explanation: Pluto's distance varies (perihelion ~4,400M km, aphelion ~7,300M km). Semi-major axis is 5,900M km (standard).
issues.fix: Change distance: 7500 to distance: 5900 for consistency

issues.explanation: Same as line 244 - duplicate entry for same mission
Closest approach is ~6.1M km from Sun's center (~696,000 km radius, so ~5.4M km from surface). 0.05M km = 50,000 km is inside the corona.
issues.fix: Change distance: 0.05 to distance: 6.1 or add note that this is from surface

issues.explanation: Moon's average distance is 384,400 km (0.384M km). Using 0.45 creates inconsistency with Apollo/Luna missions.
Joint mission with European Space Agency and Italian Space Agency. If maintaining single-color encoding, note partnership in description.
Joint mission with ESA and Canadian Space Agency

verified_correct: All major historical dates (first satellite, human, spacewalk, Moon landing)

recommendations.action: Fix Pioneer/Voyager distances to use mission destinations, not current spacecraft positions
Remove or postpone Artemis II entry (mission not yet launched)
Fix Parker Solar Probe distance or clarify measurement method
Use consistent Moon distance (0.384M km) for all lunar missions
